_descriptor_type: A type of descriptor. One of 'simulation', 'trace', and 'perf'.
_architecture: A name of the available architectures (PARAMS.<architecture>) found in scarab repository, which defines the machine parameters of the default configuration.
_experiment: Seqnum-driven branch-delay-load replay prefetcher sweep on SPEC CPU2006, crossed with fill level. Reads the per-instance <bench>_<simpt>_pf.csv (seqnum,line) recorded by bld_pf_record_spec06 and, at retire, issues MRT_DPRF prefetches for recorded lines when the retire pointer (op_num) is within --branch_load_dep_pf_lookahead of each instance's seqnum. Two 1-D sweeps sharing the la2048/cov1.0 corner: (a) lookahead at full coverage, straddling the 352-entry ROB (128 = below ROB = too late; 512/2048/8192 = increasing lead); (b) coverage at a fixed lookahead (2048), where the braddr.csv line-set gates which lines are prefetched. Every sweep point is run at BOTH fill levels: _l1 = --branch_load_dep_pf_level 0 (fill L1D) and _l2 = --branch_load_dep_pf_level 1 (fill L2/MLC). Bracketed by baseline (floor), marked_hit and perfect-D$ all_hit oracles (ceilings). REQUIRES scarab_path built from bld-prefetch-seqnum-csv and the pf.csv/braddr.csv produced by bld_pf_record_spec06 in bld_traces_kmhv3_pf.
_workload_manager: manual: run each exp scenario with all the simpoints at a time, slurm: Slurm schedules simulations based on its policy
_root_dir: Root directory mounted into the docker container home, for instance, the user's home directory
_application_dir: The path to the benchmark application directory used for tracing or simulation.
_scarab_path: The path to the scarab repository that contains the scarab binary used to run the experiment. Must be built from the bld-prefetch-seqnum-csv branch (adds --branch_load_dep_pf_replay).
_traces_dir: The local directory containing all simpointed traces
_top_simpoint: Run only top 3 simpoints stored in 'workloads_top_simp.json'. Must match bld_pf_record_spec06 so the recorded pf.csv/braddr.csv cover the simpoints the replay reads back.
_simulations: SPEC CPU2006 integer + floating-point rate suites (matches bld_pf_record_spec06 and dcache_marked_hit_vs_perfect_spec06).
_configurations: Prefetcher common params (all pf_* configs): --branch_load_dep_pf_replay 1, pf trigger stream from --branch_load_dep_pf_dir, coverage line-set from --branch_load_dep_pf_coverage_dir. Both dirs point at bld_traces_kmhv3_pf (the bld_pf_record_spec06 output); repoint pf_coverage_dir to bld_traces_kmhv3 to gate on the original braddr set. Sweep axes: --branch_load_dep_pf_lookahead (W, retire-order op_num units; ROB=352) and --branch_load_dep_pf_coverage (fraction of braddr stall cycles kept; 1.0 = no gating). Fill level: _l1 = --branch_load_dep_pf_level 0 (L1D), _l2 = --branch_load_dep_pf_level 1 (L2/MLC). L2 fills leave the L1D unpolluted but need the load to hit the freshly-filled L2 on demand; comparing _l1 vs _l2 per point isolates whether L1D pollution or L2 residency dominates.

pf_la128_cov100_l1._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L1D: W=128 < ROB(352), too-late reference.

pf_la128_cov100_l2._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L2/MLC: W=128 < ROB(352), too-late reference.

pf_la512_cov100_l1._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L1D: W=512, just above ROB.

pf_la512_cov100_l2._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L2/MLC: W=512, just above ROB.

pf_la2048_cov100_l1._params: Shared anchor, fill L1D: W=2048 (comfortable lead), cov 1.0 (no gating).

pf_la2048_cov100_l2._params: Shared anchor, fill L2/MLC: W=2048 (comfortable lead), cov 1.0 (no gating).

pf_la8192_cov100_l1._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L1D: W=8192, aggressive (watch early-eviction).

pf_la8192_cov100_l2._params: Lookahead sweep @ cov 1.0, fill L2/MLC: W=8192, aggressive. L2 is larger, so it should tolerate a longer lead than L1D before eviction.

pf_la2048_cov90_l1._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L1D: keep lines covering 90% of braddr stall cycles.

pf_la2048_cov90_l2._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L2/MLC: keep lines covering 90% of braddr stall cycles.

pf_la2048_cov50_l1._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L1D: keep lines covering 50% of braddr stall cycles.

pf_la2048_cov50_l2._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L2/MLC: keep lines covering 50% of braddr stall cycles.

pf_la2048_cov25_l1._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L1D: keep lines covering 25% of braddr stall cycles (hottest tail).

pf_la2048_cov25_l2._params: Coverage sweep @ W=2048, fill L2/MLC: keep lines covering 25% of braddr stall cycles (hottest tail).

marked_hit_cov100._params: Reference ceiling: attribution force-hit oracle (any access to a recorded line completes at L1 hit latency), full coverage. Reads braddr from the same recorded set.

all_hit._params: Reference ceiling: perfect-D$ hit-latency oracle (every load completes at L1 hit latency).

_mem: Memory scheduling and collection settings for Slurm jobs.
